settings.title: Twenty Years of First Steps and Painted Hands
settings.excerpt: Two decades on, some of the children we cared for are now bringing us children of their own.
settings.body:
This summer Little Oaks turns twenty. Over those years thousands of children have taken their first wobbly steps and made their first friends within our walls.

The moment that tells us how long it has been is a quiet one. Several parents dropping off this year were once children here themselves, and now they are trusting us with the next generation.

Twenty years of small moments

It is not the big events we remember most, but the ordinary ones repeated thousands of times. The first morning a child waves goodbye without tears. The painting carried home like treasure. The friendship that starts over a shared box of bricks. Two decades of those moments add up to something we are quietly proud of.

To celebrate, we are holding a summer fair in the garden on 4 July with cake, games, and a wall of photographs from twenty years. Old friends and new families are all welcome to join us.

What you will find at the fair

There will be plenty for everyone, current families and curious visitors alike:

  • A wall of photographs spanning all twenty years.
  • Games and activities for the little ones.
  • Cake, tea, and time to catch up with old faces.

If you were once a child here, we would especially love to see you. Bring your own little ones along and show them where it all started.
